HIV (human immunodeficiency virus) is a virus that attacks the human immune system. Unlike some other viruses, such as the common cold, HIV cannot be cleared from the body. There is no cure for HIV, but there are treatments available. Talk to your healthcare provider and learn more about a treatment option.HIV (human immunodeficiency virus) is a virus that attacks the human immune system. Column-splitter can be more than one. Each column-splitter can hold micro components (linked-list, images, videos, CTA's) and RTF's
Spacings
  • Space between the headings and the paragraph below it should be 16px.
  • Space between the paragraph and the video should be 32px.
  • Top and bottom spacings of the content blocks should be 60px in desktop and 40px in mobile.
  • Left and right spacings should be 20px in mobile.
  • The space between the columns should be 32px in desktop.
Component: 1-Column Content Block | Variant: Variant with Media and Text

Who should not take DESCOVY for PrEP?

If you think you might have been exposed to HIV before starting or while taking DESCOVY for PrEP, let a healthcare provider know right away. DESCOVY by itself is not a complete treatment for HIV—in fact, taking DESCOVY alone to treat HIV can make HIV harder to treat now and in the future. Do not take DESCOVY for PrEP if you weigh less than 77 pounds.

components.name: filter
components.snippet:
Component: Filter | Variant: Filter with static content
How it works:
  • By default, all static content is displayed.
  • When user selects a filter option, the content updates to reflect the selected criteria.
  • User can clear the selected filter to reset the content.
  • User can apply multiple filters at once.
  • Selected filters are displayed above the content area.
  • User can unselect a filter either by unchecking on the filter checkbox or by clicking on the "x" icon next to the filter label.
  • On mobile screens, the filter options are collapsed by default and can be expanded by tapping on the filter button.

Filter topics

Filter

components.name: tabs
components.snippet:
Component: Tabs | Variant: Tabs base component
  • 'eds-tabs' class is mandatory for all tabs components.
  • Below structure is the base for all variants.
  • Sitecore should be able to add/remove classes on parent level eds-tabs div
  • Make sure to provide unique ids for each tabs component in a page to avoid any conflicts.
  • Sitecore should be able to add img tag, picture tag or svg icon as per requirement inside nav-item along with text.
  • Sitecore should able to add/remove classes like font, color, etc. on nav-link button
  • Make sure to add 'active' class to the default active tab button i.e; the first tab button inside nav-tabs in below example.
  • The first tab button in each tabs component is set as active by default. You can change the active tab by updating the classes and attributes in the DOM.
  • Make sure to add 'aria-selected="true"' to the default active tab button i.e; the first tab button in below example.
  • Sitecore should be able to author values for aria-controls as per the tab pane id.
  • The id attribute value for button and tab pane aria-labelledby attribute value should match and be unique for each tabs component in a page to avoid any conflicts.
  • data-bs-target value should match with the tab pane id value.
  • The tab-pane id here should match with the aria-controls values of the corresponding tab buttons.
  • The tab-pane aria-labelledby values here should match with the id values of the corresponding tab buttons.
  • Make sure to add 'show active' classes to the default active tab pane i.e; the tab content inside first tab pane

components.name: tooltip
components.snippet:
Patients may pay as little as $0 out of pocket for TRODELVY, which includes co-payand coinsurance, up to $25,000 annually

The program only assists with the cost of TRODELVY; patient is responsible for cost-share of treatments and office visits

This option may be available if you are enrolled in government healthcare programs, such as Medicare, Medicaid, TRICARE, or VA, or are otherwise not eligible for Gilead’s co-pay program.
